title: Specification documents are machine-readable blueprints that drive code generation and AI collaboration
content:
A specification document is a configuration file that defines how a specific module in your package should behave. It serves as a blueprint describing the structure, rules, and relationships that Powerhouse uses to generate actual code.

These specification documents unlock **Specification Driven Design & Development** (SDDD):
- They are machine-readable and executable
- They serve as a shared language between developers, designers, and AI agents
- They enable precise, iterative edits
- They turn business needs into maintainable functionality

Vetra Studio currently supports specification documents for:
- **Document Models** (data structure + operations via GraphQL SDL)
- **Editors** (UI specifications)
- **Drive-apps** (document organization interfaces)
- **Subgraphs** (GraphQL API subsets)
- **Processors** (codegen and relational DB)
description: Specs define module behavior as configuration files, enabling Specification Driven Design and Development with AI agents.
